title: Lyonia macrophylla
taxonomy: Lyonia macrophylla is a plant species classified in the family Ericaceae. This family, commonly known as the heath family, comprises a diverse group of flowering plants. Lyonia macrophylla is further classified within the genus Lyonia, which consists of several species of shrubs and small trees. intro: Lyonia macrophylla, also known as the largeleaf fothergilla or mountain fothergilla, is a flowering plant species native to Eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in treating various conditions including respiratory issues and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Lyonia macrophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
